channel.title: Rollback on Mengboy Tech Notes
channel.description: Recent content in Rollback on Mengboy Tech Notes

item.title: OpenAI Responses + GitHub Actions PR Risk Gate: Automated Evals, Tiered Blocking, and One-Click Rollback
item.description:
You don’t need an AI reviewer that “sounds smart.” You need a gate that stops risky PRs before they hit main.

This post shows a production-ready minimum setup: OpenAI Responses generates structured risk output, GitHub Actions enforces tiered policies, and critical failures can trigger a one-click rollback.

item.title: OpenAI Responses Structured Outputs with Go: Schema Evolution, Bad-Case Fallbacks, and Gradual Rollback
item.description:
The hardest part of Structured Outputs is not getting JSON once. It is surviving schema changes without turning production into a small fire with excellent logs and terrible business results.

Once a Go service starts evolving prompts and response contracts, the usual failure modes show up fast: a new required field breaks older consumers, an enum expands and strict validation kills valid requests, or one bad sample drags the whole chain into retries and rollback panic.
